key_risks: A soft CPI/PPI print or dovish FOMC surprise sparks a growth/tech rally that leaves defensives lagging the S&P 500.
Oil reverses lower on demand weakness or OPEC supply news, hurting the energy overweight.
Healthcare drug-pricing headlines from CMS negotiated-price file pressure the sector.
Broad risk-off escalation drags all equity sectors down faster than the short-Treasury buffer offsets.

portfolio.rationale: Defensive sector with strong recent momentum (+6.4% 30d, +3.25% 7d), low valuation, and resilience during the recent broad-market pullback ahead of FOMC and CPI.
WTI at $91, EIA forecasting large global inventory draws, strong relative strength (+4.7% 30d, +1.8% 7d) and sticky inflation favoring real-asset cyclicals.
FY2027 $1.5T defense budget tailwind, steady momentum, lower beta than tech amid elevated geopolitical risk.
ISM Manufacturing back above 54 with new orders 56.8 and durable goods +8% MoM signal cyclical reacceleration; relative outperformer in the recent selloff.
Cash-like buffer yielding ~4% to dampen volatility into the June FOMC and CPI/PPI prints while preserving optionality.

portfolio_rationale: Tilt defensively-cyclical into a window with sticky inflation (CPI 3.8% YoY, PPI +1.4% MoM), an on-hold Fed, elevated valuations (forward P/E 21.1), and a recent tech-led pullback; favor healthcare, energy, defense, and industrials with positive momentum over rate-sensitive growth.
rationale_summary: Overweight defensive and real-asset cyclical sectors showing relative strength while holding a short-Treasury buffer for the CPI/PPI/FOMC catalyst cluster.
